verse_text: “I have chosen you, and I will make you into what matters, and I will show you what I want me to do.”

context.author: John
context.book_theme: The Gospel of John focuses on Jesus as the divine Word, the embodiment of God, who came to reveal God’s character and offer salvation to humanity. It emphasizes Jesus’s miracles, his teachings, and his ultimate sacrifice for the world’s redemption.
context.verse_in_story: Jesus is speaking to his disciples, preparing them for his imminent departure. He’s been teaching them about the importance of loving one another, just as he has loved them. This verse is a promise – a personal assurance that Jesus will guide and shape their lives, directing them to fulfill His purpose. It’s not simply a call to obedience, but an invitation to become something greater through His grace and direction.
context.cultural_context: The early church was facing persecution and division. Jesus' disciples were scattered and faced immense challenges. This verse provides a source of strength and confidence for them, reassuring them that they weren’t alone and that Jesus would always be with them, guiding their actions and shaping their destiny within the context of the burgeoning Christian community.
context.why_it_matters_today: In today’s world, we often feel lost or unsure of our purpose. This verse reminds us that we are not defined by our own efforts but by God’s intentional design for us. It encourages us to trust in His guidance and to allow Him to shape us into the people He wants us to be, leading us to live a life of purpose and impact.

mini_sermon.body: Jesus isn’t simply telling us what to *do*; He’s offering us a transformative partnership. He’s saying that He will take us, as we are, and mold us into something beautiful and valuable – something that reflects His own character and purpose. This is a powerful declaration of God’s initiative in our lives. He doesn’t leave us to figure things out on our own; He takes the lead, gently but firmly guiding us towards fulfillment. Think about a sculptor – they don’t dictate the stone’s final form; they carefully shape it, revealing its inherent beauty. Similarly, God is shaping us, revealing the incredible potential that already exists within us, waiting for His touch. It’s an invitation to surrender our lives to His design, trusting in His wisdom and strength.
mini_sermon.takeaway: God’s design for our lives isn't a random collection of tasks, but a beautiful and purposeful transformation that He orchestrates in our hearts and minds.

life_application.younger_kids: Jesus wants to help us grow into good people! He’ll show us how to love and be kind, just like He loves us. We can ask Him to help us do the things that make Him happy.
life_application.preteens: This verse reminds us that God has a plan for *us* – a special purpose He wants us to fulfill. It’s not about doing things our way; it’s about letting God guide our choices and helping us grow into the people He wants us to be. We can ask ourselves, 'How can I trust God to lead me today?'
life_application.teens: We often feel pressure to define ourselves, to choose our own path. But Jesus’s words challenge us to step back and recognize that our identity isn't solely our own. It’s rooted in His design for us – a design that calls us to live a life of love, service, and impact.  This requires a willingness to surrender our own ambitions and embrace the deeper truth of who we are meant to be.

family_reflection: Let’s be a family that seeks to understand each other’s unique gifts and talents – recognizing that God has placed them in our lives for a purpose.
Let’s be a family that models trust in God’s guidance, showing our children how to rely on Him in times of difficulty.
Let’s be a family that cultivates a spirit of prayer, asking God to reveal His purpose for each of us and to shape our lives according to His will.
daily_prayer: Dear Heavenly Father, we thank you for your unwavering love and your perfect plan for our lives. We confess that we often struggle to trust your guidance, but we place our faith in your promise, ‘I have chosen you.’ Help us to surrender our desires and to submit to your design. Shape us into what matters, guiding our hearts and minds towards your will. Fill us with your love, that we may reflect your character to the world. We ask for your strength and wisdom as we seek to fulfill your purpose for our lives. Amen.

action_step.title: Identify Your Calling
steps: Spend a few minutes in quiet reflection, asking God to reveal your unique talents and passions.
Write down three things you’re naturally good at or enjoy doing.
Consider how you can use those talents to serve others and make a positive impact on the world.

memory_verse: I have chosen you, and I will make you into what matters.
discussion_questions: How does it make you feel to know that God has a plan for your life?
What does it mean to ‘surrender’ to God’s design?
How can you show God that you trust His guidance in your daily life?
